page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-48xCPU-384GB is a memory-optimized server instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 48 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-threaded capabilities. The single-core CPU performance is poor, placing in the bottom 10% on stress-ng benchmarks, and single-threaded decompression tasks remain average. However, multi-threaded execution is robust, with bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, Passmark CPU scores, and Passmark CPU physics tests all ranking in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is consistently average across both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks, while LLM inference prompt processing for small models also registers in the top 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU allocation model offers a high density of compute cores and memory resources without the premium of dedicated hardware. This makes the instance suitable for workloads that can tolerate shared CPU scheduling but require substantial memory capacity. Tradeoffs include the lack of local storage and weak text generation speeds for larger models. This configuration is best suited for multi-threaded memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and parallel data processing tasks.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-48xCPU-384GB is a KVM-based, memory-optimized server instance featuring 48 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz on an x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding 8.0 GB per core, with no bundled local storage or GPUs. Benchmark data indicates a clear performance tradeoff: single-core CPU performance is poor, ranking in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-threaded capabilities are strong, with Passmark CPU and multi-threaded decompression benchmarks placing in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth and database operations perform at average levels. Given its shared CPU allocation and high memory density, this instance offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-heavy, highly parallelized workloads such as database operations, multi-threaded data processing, and small-model LLM prompt processing, provided that dedicated CPU cores and local storage are not required.

tagline: A memory-optimized shared-core instance delivering strong multi-threaded performance and high memory density for parallelized data workloads.

Strong multi-threaded performance, ranking in the top 25% for Passmark CPU tests
Poor single-core CPU performance, placing in the bottom 10% on stress-ng
No bundled local storage or GPU hardware included
Suited for database operations, parallel data processing, and memory-intensive tasks
